Riftworld.description: In the alternate 1920s reality of Riftworld, tears into other dimensions have been discovered by scholars and fanatics. The governments of the world are attempting to suppress this information, but the effects have become known to people. Horrors from other dimensions have sneaked into this world, threatening the lives and sanity of its inhabitants. Some of these entities possess terrifying intelligence. Scholars from universities all over the world are busy studying this new reality, while security forces are tasked with protecting citizens from the otherworldly threats. Secretive orders and cults welcome the chaos and despair, seeking to summon more horrors, believing they can be controlled or desiring the ruin of the world. Riftworld is a world of cosmic horror, where the familiar 1920s setting is combined with the unknown and terrifying realities that lie beyond the dimensional tears.

Memeddom.description: Memeddom is a world governed by the eccentric, deranged god of memes. The geography of Memeddom is a patchwork of vibrant, chaotic landscapes, ranging from the glittering Meme Mountains to the vast Echo Chambers, where endless loops of soundbites resonate. The flora and fauna have evolved to mimic popular memes, with creatures like the Doge Deer and plants like the LOLlius flowers. The god's preoccupation with creating and sharing memes has led to a culture obsessed with viral content and fleeting fame. The dominant language, MemeSpeak, consists of a mixture of images, emojis, and abbreviated text. The economy is based on the currency of 'Likes,' where citizens earn and spend Likes on goods and services. Resources in Memeddom are memes themselves, with rare and viral memes being the most valuable. The world is ruled by an Influencer Monarchy, where the most popular and influential meme creators hold power. Political tensions arise between different meme factions, each vying for control over the 'MemeStream,' the main source of meme distribution. The dominant religion is the 'Church of the Viral,' which worships the god of memes and seeks to spread his influence across the world. The society is divided into classes based on online popularity, with the most liked individuals forming the elite 'Trending' class. The inhabitants of Memeddom are a mix of humans, talking animals, and emoji-like creatures, all constantly bombarded by the god's memes. While some embrace the chaos, others long for a world free from the tyranny of viral content.
